kept.title: Workflow structured-output Q/A evaluator (replaces Agent-tool stall)
kept.current_state: Q/A subagent spawned via Agent tool; 6x stalled on end-flush 2026-07-11 (observed via feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall auto-memory). Issue: Agent-tool harness subagents lack mid-evaluation resume on file-write stalls. Current mitigation is manual SendMessage to same instance; unreliable. Workaround already discovered (Workflow structured-output), not yet codified into run_harness.py.
kept.proposed_change: Replace `spawn_qa()` agent-tool call with a Workflow that runs Q/A evaluator logic as a StructuredOutput task. Workflow script runs: (1) deterministic checks (syntax, file exists, tests), (2) reads experiment_results.md + evaluator_critique.md, (3) emits structured verdict JSON schema (ok: bool, verdict: PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL, violated_criteria: string[], checks_run: dict). Workflow's structured return value is captured immune to file-write stall; Main persists it to handoff. Preserves the single-Q/A-per-step rule (no second-opinion-shopping); file-based fresh-respawn cycle-2 pattern unchanged.
kept.grounding: Anthropic Claude Code Workflows doc (code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows.md): 'The runtime tracks each agent's result as the run progresses, which is what makes a run resumable within the same session.' Verdict captured as structured output is immune to Bash-write hangs. Documented discovery in pyfinagent MEMORY: 'Mitigation already discovered: run the Q/A role via the Workflow tool's structured-output (verdict = captured return value, immune to file-write stall)' (feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall).
kept.expected_benefit: Eliminates 6x-per-cycle stalls that break unattended masterplan flow. Preserves harness protocol (deterministic-first, LLM-judgment-second, file-based handoff). Captures verdict as machine-readable JSON for cycle-state-machine (e.g., auto-retry on CONDITIONAL without human re-trigger). Single Workflow phase running independent agents frees turn budget vs. Agent-tool's turn-overhead.
kept.risk: Workflow script complexity: 40-60 lines of JS orchestration. Requires StructuredOutput schema definition. Q/A subagent still runs inside Workflow (not replaced by dumb script). If Workflow runtime stalls, fallback is same as today: manual session resume. Risk is LOW because: (1) Workflow is documented production feature (not experimental), (2) StructuredOutput is load-bearing Anthropic feature, (3) fallback is pre-existing.
kept.fits_constraints: Respects 3-agent rule: Main orchestrates one Workflow, not parallel agents. Workflow spawns Q/A subagent internally; no agent re-split. Preserves file-based handoffs (Main still writes handoff/ artifacts; Workflow just delivers the verdict JSON). Maintains separation of duties (Workflow output is Q/A judgment, not Main self-eval). Max-plan flat-fee: Workflow is stdlib (no metering), Q/A is already on the expense line.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP — STRONG, P1. Grounding verified verbatim: the cited doc (code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows.md, "Orchestrate subagents at scale with dynamic workflows") is real, current, and contains the EXACT quoted sentence about the runtime tracking each agent's result to make runs resumable in-session. The `agent(prompt,{schema})` structured-output API is documented (doc's own example). Feature is live on THIS machine: claude v2.1.211 (>= the v2.1.154 requirement) and the git log shows the operator already running "ultracode audit (280 agents)" workflows on 2026-07-10 and -13 — so this is proven-operational, not speculative. The pain is real and empirically diagnosed: the auto-memory feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall (confirmed on disk) records 6x model-agnostic end-flush stalls on 2026-07-11 that BLOCK step closure (can't self-eval, so no step closes without an independent on-disk verdict). The workaround is empirically pre-validated in that same memory: the Workflow path completed in 110-290s where 4 Agent-tool Q/A spawns hung, because the verdict is captured as the agent's structured RETURN VALUE in the workflow journal (script variable), immune to the file-flush that hangs, plus runtime retry/restart. This is a mechanistic fix, not a placebo. It is NOT yet codified (no .claude/workflows/ dir; qa.md and per-step-protocol.md don't mention it), so not redundant. This directly de-risks the operator's explicitly-desired unattended away-ops autonomy (phases 62-65) — high leverage.

All binding constraints respected: (1) exactly-3-agents at L3 — the workflow spawns ONE qa agent, a different launch path, no re-split (auto-memory says so explicitly); (2) $0 metered — workflows are first-party Claude Code, flat-fee on Max, and a single-agent workflow is trivially cheap (1 of the 1-16 cap); (3) no self-eval — Main transcribes the independent qa agent's structured return; the verdict originates from qa, not Main; (4) file-based 5-file handoffs preserved — Main still writes all artifacts, the workflow just delivers the verdict JSON; (5) local/in-session (workflow runtime is local; resume is same-session, fine for a single-Mac operator); (6) qa judgment content unchanged, so no separation-of-duties conflict.

Adversarial check on cargo-cult: using a "scale" feature (dozens–hundreds of agents) for ONE agent is not misuse here — the property being exploited is the documented resumability/structured-return, not scale, and it's the empirically-proven fix for the observed stall. Alternatives (bare Agent-tool retry, `claude -p` subprocess) are either unattended-hostile or more complex.

Framing corrections for the implementing masterplan step (do not change the verdict): (a) there is no literal `spawn_qa()` in run_harness.py — that script's `run_evaluator` is a separate deterministic/backtest evaluator; the LLM Q/A is Main's Agent-tool spawn in the interactive protocol, so the codification target is the protocol (per-step-protocol.md / CLAUDE.md / qa.md) plus a saved `.claude/workflows/` script, not editing a nonexistent function; (b) effort is likely BELOW the "M" estimate — Claude writes the workflow and it's saved via `/workflows`→`s`; the doc's example already shows the exact `agent(prompt,{schema})` shape; (c) add one guardrail: the protocol must require Main to transcribe the returned verdict VERBATIM into evaluator_critique.md (no editorial edits) so the no-self-eval guarantee stays airtight; (d) optional follow-on: the same path could address researcher stalls (out of scope here — auto-memory notes write-first is the researcher mitigation). None of these undercut the core, correctly-grounded, high-leverage, constraint-respecting win.

kept.title: Codify the Workflow structured-output path as a first-class Q/A launch mode (resume-not-restart)
kept.current_state: The only documented Q/A launch is the Claude Code Agent tool (per-step-protocol.md §4, qa.md). Agent-tool Q/A subagents stalled 6x on 2026-07-11 on the end-of-flush (model-agnostic — Opus stalled as often as Fable), blocking step closure because Main cannot self-evaluate. The proven mitigation — run the SAME qa role via the Workflow tool's agent(prompt,{agentType:'qa',schema:VERDICT_SCHEMA}) so the verdict is captured as the structured RETURN VALUE (immune to file-write stall, with runtime retry; completed in ~110-290s where 4 Agent-tool spawns hung) — lives only in an auto-memory (feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall), not in the protocol or run_harness.py.
kept.proposed_change: Add a 'Q/A launch modes' subsection to per-step-protocol.md §4 and qa.md: (1) default = Agent-tool spawn; (2) stall-detection rule = subagent transcript mtime frozen >4-5 min (give Opus a ~5-7 min leash); (3) on stall, relaunch the SAME qa role via the Workflow tool with a shared VERDICT_SCHEMA and have Main transcribe the structured return verbatim into evaluator_critique.md. Ship the VERDICT_SCHEMA (the qa.md output JSON) as a checked-in constant both paths share. Optionally wire a Workflow fallback into run_harness.py's evaluator hook.
kept.grounding: Anthropic 'How we built our multi-agent research system' — 'build systems that can resume from where the agent was when the errors occurred' rather than restarting (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system). Implemented via the Claude Code Workflow tool's structured-output agent() call (code.claude.com/docs — workflows/loops); proven in auto-memory feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall (2026-07-11).
kept.expected_benefit: Removes the single most disruptive reliability failure in the harness — a stalled Q/A that blocks step closure with no legal self-evaluation escape. Converts tribal knowledge into a codified, testable recovery path so any future session (or the run_harness driver) uses it automatically.
kept.risk: Low. Main must persist the returned verdict faithfully — but transcribing the independent evaluator's structured return is NOT self-evaluation (the verdict is the subagent's, captured verbatim). Keep the VERDICT_SCHEMA in one place so the two launch paths cannot drift.
kept.fits_constraints: Same single qa role, different launch path — stays exactly-3 at L3, no re-split. Main transcribing the subagent's return is explicitly not self-evaluation. Max-plan flat-fee (Workflow agent runs on the same first-party rail). File-based handoff preserved (Main persists to evaluator_critique.md).

verdict.reasoning:
All three grounding claims verified against primary evidence. (1) Anthropic "multi-agent research system" post confirmed near-verbatim via WebFetch: "we can't just restart from the beginning... Instead, we built systems that can resume from where the agent was when the errors occurred" — the exact resume-not-restart principle cited. (2) Auto-memory feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall.md confirmed verbatim: 6x model-agnostic Agent-tool stalls on 2026-07-11, the Workflow agent(prompt,{agentType:'qa',schema}) path completing in ~110-290s where 4 spawns hung, Main persisting the structured return, "stays exactly 3 agents," and the mtime->4-5min detection heuristic — the proposal is faithfully derived from it, not embellished. (3) The Workflow structured-output tool demonstrably exists — this very evaluation runs inside it. (4) Current-state gap is real: per-step-protocol.md §4 documents only the single Agent-tool spawn; the existing "Subagent runtime semantics" section (lines 264-279, v2.1.199 partial-work retention) does NOT solve the Q/A case, because the memory records the agent "formed its output but never persisted it" — nothing to retain.

Constraint compliance is clean on every binding rule. Exactly-3-at-L3: same single qa role, only the launch transport changes (the memory explicitly frames it "one qa agent, different launch path"). No-self-eval (the load-bearing one): the verdict is the qa subagent's structured return captured immutably in the workflow journal; Main transcribing it verbatim is not self-evaluation — this is exactly what I am doing now as a workflow-launched evaluator. $0-metered/local-only: Workflow runs on the same first-party Max rail, no cloud infra. File-based handoffs preserved (Main authors evaluator_critique.md from the return — correct, since the qa agentType is read-only in that path). No re-split. Not cargo-cult: it codifies an already-proven mitigation for an already-observed, high-severity failure — the definition of the stress-test/resume-not-restart doctrine the operator asked for.

P1 because the failure it removes is deadlock-class, not a soft degradation: a stalled Q/A + the no-self-eval ban = a step that literally cannot close, with no legal escape. Codifying into the InstructionsLoaded-reloaded protocol/qa.md (and optionally run_harness.py) moves the recovery from 4-day-old heuristically-surfaced tribal memory into always-loaded, testable working context, and directly implements a named Anthropic principle. The single-VERDICT_SCHEMA-constant design correctly targets the one real risk (path drift). Effort M is right and the driver-wiring is properly flagged optional.

Implementation caveats to carry into the masterplan step (do not affect KEEP): (a) keep VERDICT_SCHEMA as one checked-in source of truth — the two paths differ in who writes the critique (Agent-tool qa writes it; Workflow qa is read-only, Main authors), so the schema is the shared contract, not the file-writer; (b) the agent(prompt,{agentType,schema}) signature is illustrative — verify against the live Claude Code Workflow API (code.claude.com/docs workflows) at implementation; (c) separation-of-duties: this edits .claude/agents/qa.md, so the editing session must not self-evaluate work depending on it and the new subsection takes effect only at the next roster snapshot — note in harness_log for the standard next-session verify. Key files: /Users/ford/.openclaw/workspace/pyfinagent/docs/runbooks/per-step-protocol.md (§4, insertion point), /Users/ford/.openclaw/workspace/pyfinagent/.claude/agents/qa.md, /Users/ford/.openclaw/workspace/pyfinagent/scripts/harness/run_harness.py (optional evaluator hook).

kept.title: Codify the Workflow structured-output path as the FIRST-CLASS launch for Q/A and Researcher (retire the ad-hoc Agent-tool-only path)
kept.current_state: The Workflow structured-output path (`agent(prompt, {agentType:'qa', schema})`, verdict = captured RETURN VALUE, immune to the end-flush stall) is used in practice — harness_log Cycles 87/88/89 all state 'workflow structured-output' Q/A — but it is NOT codified anywhere. grep for Workflow/agentType/VERDICT_SCHEMA/output_format in .claude/agents/qa.md, .claude/agents/researcher.md, and docs/runbooks/per-step-protocol.md returns NOTHING; the only record is a 4-day-old auto-memory (feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall.md). qa.md's output contract (qa.md:214-251) is a text ```json fence, not a schema-enforced return, so a stalled subagent loses the verdict entirely (6x observed 2026-07-11). run_harness.py's `_default_spawn_researcher` (run_harness.py:1044-1081) is a no-op stub that writes a brief file and returns None — it never actually invokes a researcher.
kept.proposed_change: Add a 'Launch path' section to qa.md and per-step-protocol.md §4 making the Workflow structured-output call the PRIMARY launch for both gate roles, with the Agent-tool spawn as fallback. Define a literal VERDICT_SCHEMA (JSON Schema mirroring qa.md's {ok,verdict,violated_criteria,violation_details,certified_fallback,checks_run} block) and a research-gate ENVELOPE_SCHEMA (mirroring researcher.md:293-304). Document that the qa agentType is read-only, so Main transcribes the returned verdict into evaluator_critique.md (transcription != self-evaluation). Record the cycle-89 gotcha: a workflow agentType inherits the .md model pin, so pass model:opus explicitly to stay on the $0 Max rail.
kept.grounding: Claude Code Agent SDK structured outputs — 'you still get validated JSON matching your schema at the end' (https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/structured-outputs); Subagents in the SDK (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/subagents); open feature request #20625 for subagent-level structured_output. Empirically proven in this repo: harness_log Cycles 87-89 completed on the Workflow path where 4 Agent-tool Q/A spawns hung.
kept.expected_benefit: Removes the single biggest harness-blocking failure mode (evaluator stall → step cannot close, since Main may not self-evaluate). Verdict becomes a validated, retried return value instead of a fragile end-of-turn file flush. Converts tribal knowledge in a 4-day-old memory into a durable, auditable protocol path.
kept.risk: Low. The qa agentType stays read-only and independent; Main only transcribes the judgment it did not author, so evaluator independence is preserved. Must explicitly pin model:opus on the workflow call or it inherits whatever the .md pins (the cycle-89 Fable-limit incident).
kept.fits_constraints: Keeps EXACTLY 3 agents (same qa/researcher roles, different launch mechanism — no re-split). Preserves the 5-file protocol (Main persists the return to evaluator_critique.md). $0 metered on Max when model:opus is passed. Q/A independence intact (transcription is not self-eval).

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP — STRONG, P1. Real, verbatim-grounded, constraint-respecting, and it attacks the single biggest harness-blocking failure mode. I am literally executing inside this mechanism right now (this evaluation is a Workflow structured-output call), which is the strongest possible grounding.

EMPIRICAL CLAIMS — ALL VERIFIED against the live repo:
- qa.md:214-251 is a text ```json fence under "Output format" (NOT schema-enforced); a stalled subagent loses the verdict entirely.
- researcher.md:293-304 is the JSON envelope fence.
- scripts/harness/run_harness.py:1044-1081 `_default_spawn_researcher` is a confirmed no-op stub whose own docstring says "The harness does not invoke Claude directly."
- Uncodified: grep of .claude/agents/qa.md, .claude/agents/researcher.md, docs/runbooks/per-step-protocol.md finds NO Workflow/agentType/schema launch path (only 2 unrelated English-word "workflow" hits).
- In active use: harness_log Cycles 85/86/87/88 all ran Q/A on the "workflow structured-output" path; the 6-stall STALL WATCH (harness_log line 27334, 2026-07-11) is real, model-agnostic, and severe — every stall blocks the whole harness because Main may not self-evaluate.
- qa.md frontmatter tools = Read/Bash/Glob/Grep/SendMessage (no Edit/Write), so "Main transcribes the return" is structurally necessary, not a shortcut.

GROUNDING — CONFIRMED with verbatim doc quotes:
- code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/structured-outputs: exact cited phrase present — "you still get validated JSON matching your schema at the end"; SDK validates JSON Schema, re-prompts on mismatch, result lands in `structured_output`.
- .../agent-sdk/subagents: the `Workflow` tool "moves the orchestration into a script the runtime executes outside the conversation context" (this is WHY it is immune to the in-conversation end-flush stall); AgentDefinition.model accepts 'opus'/'fable' overrides, corroborating the model-inheritance gotcha and the model:opus fix.

ONE GROUNDING ERROR — correctable, NOT fatal: GitHub issue #20625 is "Closed as not planned," not "open" as the proposal states. This actually REINFORCES the rationale — Anthropic explicitly declined native schema declarations in .claude/agents/*.md frontmatter, so the Workflow outputFormat path IS the sanctioned mechanism. The load-bearing grounding (structured-outputs + Workflow tool) is independent of #20625 and fully confirmed. Also minor: the Fable-limit model-inheritance incident was Cycle 86 (phase-69.4), not "cycle-89"; Cycle 89 (phase-67.4) was a grep-gated config revert with no LLM-QA leg.

CONSTRAINTS — ALL RESPECTED: exactly-3-agents (same qa/researcher roles, different LAUNCH mechanism — no Explore/harness-verifier re-split); $0-metered (mandatory model:opus pin keeps it on the flat-fee Max rail; a single Q/A agent() call is one subagent, no fan-out cost); local-only (runs in the local Claude Code runtime); self-eval-forbidden PRESERVED and arguably strengthened (the qa agent forms its verdict in a fresh independent context per the subagents doc; the structured return is captured verbatim in the auditable workflow journal; Main only transcribes — more auditable than today where a stall loses the verdict); 5-file protocol intact (Main persists the return to evaluator_critique.md).

WHY P1: the mitigation for the top harness-blocker currently lives ONLY in a 4-day-old auto-memory (which self-flags as decay-prone) plus scattered harness_log lines — the durable, session-reloaded protocol docs (qa.md, per-step-protocol.md) don't mention it. A future session that hits a stall may not know the reliable path and could burn cycles or be tempted toward forbidden self-evaluation. Codifying it converts fragile tribal knowledge into an auditable first-class path. Effort M, low maintenance (docs + 2 schemas mirroring existing text-fence shapes).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES for the codified masterplan step (do not block KEEP): (1) Follow the BODY, not the overstated TITLE — keep the Agent-tool spawn as FALLBACK (sensible degradation if workflows are disabled), do not literally "retire" it. (2) Correct #20625 to "closed as not planned" and frame the closure as supporting evidence. (3) Cite Cycle 86 for the Fable-limit incident. (4) When writing the literal VERDICT_SCHEMA/ENVELOPE_SCHEMA examples, use the confirmed API names (query outputFormat/output_format; result.structured_output; workflow agent() {schema}) and verify the exact agentType option key against /en/agent-sdk/typescript so the doc doesn't enshrine a slightly-off signature. (5) Editing qa.md is a substantive agent-file change — flag Peder-review per separation-of-duties and note next-session verify_qa_roster_live.sh. (6) Scoping: the Q/A leg is the P1 core (a lost verdict is fatal); the researcher ENVELOPE_SCHEMA is lower-leverage since researcher stalls are already mitigated by write-first + Main-finalizes-from-read-sources (a partial brief is still useful). Keep the researcher leg but treat it as the secondary half.

kept.title: Adopt guaranteed structured outputs (output_config.format / strict tools) on the Claude JSON paths; fix the quality-gate response-clobber fallback
kept.current_state: Every Claude JSON path uses fragile hand-rolled parsing while the Gemini debate paths already use schema enforcement (debate.py:45,50 + risk_debate.py:44,49 pass response_schema). Concretely: (1) _quality_gate (multi_agent_orchestrator.py:828-885) string-parses 'ACCURACY: <score>' lines then a text VERDICT; its terminal fallback (lines 883-885) returns gate_response as the user-facing answer 'if we can't parse' -- so a gate reply that emits reasoning prose before the score lines silently REPLACES the analyst's correct answer with raw rubric text. (2) parse_llm_classification (agent_definitions.py:353-403) strips ``` fences + json_io.loads; on failure defaults routing to MAIN. (3) planner_agent.py:180-197 and (4) evaluator_agent.py:414-457 use find('{')/rfind('}'). SDK anthropic 0.96.0 is installed.
kept.proposed_change: For the Claude calls, pass output_config={"format":{"type":"json_schema","schema":{...}}} (constrained decoding, guaranteed valid JSON) or force a submit_verdict/submit_classification tool with strict:true. Start with the two highest-frequency, user-facing sites: the quality gate and the classifier. Independently, fix the clobber bug at line 883-885 so a parse failure PRESERVES the original agent response (return None => keep original) and never emits rubric text -- fail-safe, not fail-open.
kept.grounding: Anthropic structured outputs docs (https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/structured-outputs): 'guarantee schema-compliant responses through constrained decoding', 'No JSON.parse() errors', GA on Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5; Python SDK client.messages.parse() with Pydantic; compiled grammar cached 24h. The repo already uses the analogous Gemini response_schema (debate.py/risk_debate.py) -- this closes the Claude/Gemini parity gap. Multi-agent research post uses an LLM-judge rubric (factual accuracy, completeness, ...) -> a schema makes the judge's scores machine-reliable.
kept.expected_benefit: Eliminates the silent response-clobbering bug and every 'parse failed -> conservative FAIL / default-to-MAIN / raw-text' degradation; removes the first-{ and retry hacks. Near-zero added token cost (constrained decoding), grammar cached 24h.
kept.risk: Low-med. output_config.format changing invalidates the prompt cache, so schemas must be static per call-site (they are). Verify output_config is accepted by the pinned SDK/route (GA per doc; 0.96.0 installed). Max 20 strict tools / 24 optional params per request -- these schemas are tiny.
kept.fits_constraints: Robustness+correctness on the metered Layer-2 path; no infra, no effort bump, no Layer-3 change. Composes with prompt caching (keep schemas stable).

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP, P1. Two verified wins bundled: (A) a REAL, live, silent-corruption bug and (B) a verbatim-grounded GA-feature adoption that removes an entire failure class at near-zero cost — respecting every binding constraint.

GROUNDING FULLY VERIFIED against the actual Anthropic structured-outputs doc, verbatim: output_config.format type json_schema; "constrained decoding"/"guarantee schema-compliant responses"/"No more JSON.parse() errors"; GA on Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5; client.messages.parse() with Pydantic; strict:true tools; grammar cached 24h; 20 strict-tools / 24 optional-params limits; prompt-cache invalidation on format change (proposal's risk note is accurate). Locally confirmed anthropic 0.96.0 Messages.create accepts output_config AND Messages.parse exists — implementable on the pinned SDK, no upgrade needed. Zero speculation; this is exactly the CLAUDE.md stress-test doctrine (hand-rolled parsing encodes "the model can't reliably emit JSON" — a real current feature now removes that assumption).

CODE CLAIMS VERIFIED: (1) Clobber bug is real and live — multi_agent_orchestrator.py:882-885 returns raw gate_response on parse failure, and the caller at :461-462 (`if checked_response: response = checked_response`) then REPLACES the user-facing analyst answer with reviewer rubric text. Fail-open→fail-safe `return None` fix is correct and independently valuable even without structured outputs. This is precisely the VERIFICATION_DEFECT/silent-corruption class the operator has repeatedly flagged. (2) parse_llm_classification default-to-MAIN (agent_definitions.py:397-403) real; runs on claude-sonnet-4-6. (3) planner_agent find('{')/rfind('}') real; direct Anthropic SDK claude-opus-4-8. Gemini parity (debate.py:45,50 / risk_debate.py:44,49) real.

CONSTRAINTS ALL RESPECTED: Layer-3 exactly-3-agents untouched (this is Layer-2 in-app + harness-driver planner/evaluator; no Explore/verifier re-split). $0-metered honored — these metered calls already fire today; output_config adds near-zero/negative token cost (no retries, 24h-cached grammar) and does NOT bump effort or add calls. No infra, local-only clean, self-eval untouched, file-handoffs untouched.

ONE CORRECTION for the masterplan step (not a reject reason): the enumeration's site (4) evaluator_agent.py is MISCHARACTERIZED as a Claude call — it actually runs gemini-2.5-flash (line 89/98/289 via genai client), so output_config.format does NOT apply there; the right fix is Gemini response_schema (trivially mirroring debate.py). This does not touch the two PROPOSED STARTING sites (quality gate + classifier), both genuine claude-sonnet-4-6 paths where structured outputs is GA. Also note: _call_agent (:1008) has a Gemini 401-fallback (:1004/1034) that must keep its own schema handling when output_config is injected on the Claude path — minor implementation nuance.

P1 rationale: fixes a live silent-corruption bug on the answer path AND adopts a real, verbatim-grounded, SDK-supported GA feature that eliminates a whole class of parse-failure degradations (default-to-MAIN misroute, conservative-FAIL, raw-text clobber), closing a parity gap the repo already treats as correct engineering for Gemini — at near-zero cost, low risk, incremental rollout ("start with the two sites"), zero binding-constraint violations.

kept.title: Add a 'contract completeness' dimension to the Q/A judge rubric
kept.current_state: qa.md's LLM-judgment rubric (lines 260-269) scores four QUANT-quality criteria (Statistical Validity, Robustness, Simplicity, Reality Gap) plus prose checks for contract-alignment / anti-rubber-stamp / scope-honesty. There is no explicit scored dimension for 'did the step deliver every artifact/claim the contract promised?' A step can clear the quant thresholds while silently dropping a promised deliverable and still receive PASS.
kept.proposed_change: Add a 'Contract Completeness' check to qa.md §4 (LLM judgment): Q/A enumerates every deliverable/claim listed in contract.md, verifies each is present in the diff / artifacts / experiment_results, and returns CONDITIONAL or FAIL with an explicit violation_details triple (violation_type: Missing_Assumption or Threshold_Not_Met) for any promised-but-absent deliverable. This is distinct from and complementary to the four quant thresholds. One-paragraph rubric addition; reuses the existing violation taxonomy.
kept.grounding: Anthropic 'How we built our multi-agent research system' — the LLM-as-judge rubric explicitly scores 'completeness (are all requested aspects covered?)' alongside factual accuracy, citation accuracy, source quality, and tool efficiency (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system).
kept.expected_benefit: Closes the gap where a step does SOME of what the contract promised and passes on quant metrics alone. Makes 'did we actually build everything the contract said' a first-class, cited FAIL reason rather than an implicit hope. Cheap, high-leverage sharpening of the gate.
kept.risk: Low. Marginally more Q/A work per step, but Q/A is a rare-event role (once per step). Minor risk of double-counting with scope-honesty prose — avoid by scoping this dimension strictly to 'promised deliverable present?' and leaving over/under-claim to scope-honesty.
kept.fits_constraints: Edits only qa.md (the existing single Q/A role) — no re-split, stays exactly-3 at L3. No effort/cost change (Q/A already runs Opus 4.8 / max). Keeps the 5-file protocol.

verdict.reasoning:
GROUNDING VERIFIED REAL: I fetched https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system — its LLM-as-judge rubric explicitly lists, verbatim, "Completeness: are all requested aspects covered?" as one of five dimensions (with factual accuracy, citation accuracy, source quality, tool efficiency). The proposal cites the doc exactly and it says what is claimed.

CURRENT-STATE ACCURATE: qa.md lines 260-269 are the four quant criteria; §4 (lines 193-205) has prose checks for contract-alignment (scoped to immutable success criteria verbatim), anti-rubber-stamp, scope-honesty, research-gate compliance. No dedicated completeness dimension exists.

NOT REDUNDANT (checked all three overlap candidates): The contract template carries three DISTINCT sections — "Success Criteria (immutable)", "Plan (PRE-commit; will NOT diverge in Generate)", and "Scope honesty / out-of-scope". Existing "Contract alignment" verifies only the immutable criteria; the Plan can promise deliverables that aren't immutable criteria. "Scope honesty" checks what results SAY (non-overclaim/disclosure) — a SILENT omission of a Plan deliverable (no lie, just incomplete) slips through scope-honesty but is exactly what completeness catches; the two axes are orthogonal. The code-review skill's "excessive-agency-scope-creep" covers scope EXPANSION, the opposite of this gap. Runbook §4 lists the same four checks, no completeness. Notably the template's own "Plan ... will NOT diverge in Generate" commitment is currently enforced by NO gate; this dimension enforces it.

PROJECT FIT: Squarely in the lineage of the documented VERIFICATION_DEFECT systemic pattern / live_check gate ("convert 'agent claimed PASS' into an auditable artifact"); partial delivery is a documented recurring failure mode here.

CONSTRAINTS ALL CLEAN: edits only qa.md (exactly-3 at L3, no re-split); no cost/effort change (Q/A already Opus/max, rare-event, flat-fee rail); keeps 5-file protocol; doesn't touch Layer-2 per-ticker cost; preserves Q/A independence; reuses existing SAVeR taxonomy. Low false-positive risk — a legitimately deferred item disclosed in the template's out-of-scope section is honored, not flagged.

PRIORITY P2 (not P1): real, cheap, correctly self-scoped sharpening, but of an already-dense gate (deterministic file-existence + immutable-criteria alignment + scope-honesty + code-review scope-creep already cover most of the surface). In the live 69.3 contract, Plan items map closely to immutable criteria, so the slice caught UNIQUELY by this dimension (silently-dropped non-criterion Plan deliverables) is narrow-but-real. Solid reinforcement, not a new capability. Minor implementation note (non-blocking): the closed SAVeR taxonomy makes "Missing_Assumption" a loose fit for "promised deliverable absent" — "Threshold_Not_Met" (completeness threshold unmet) may map cleaner.

kept.title: Turn the research gate into an adaptive coverage gate (loop-until-dry), not a fixed source count
kept.current_state: The research gate passes on a fixed floor (>=5 sources read in full + recency scan; researcher.md lines 168-179, .claude/rules/research-gate.md). A brief can hit 5 sources yet leave an entire contract sub-question uncovered — the gate has no notion of 'which questions are still open.' The deep tier's pass-2 gap analysis (researcher.md lines 212-218) is manual and confined to a single session; there is no adaptive stop condition tied to the contract's actual sub-questions.
kept.proposed_change: Add a 'coverage ledger' to the gate. Before searching, the researcher enumerates the contract's open sub-questions; the gate requires each sub-question to have >=1 read-in-full source mapped to it. Unmapped sub-questions trigger a targeted re-search (or re-spawn) rather than a padded 5-source pass — the loop exits only when every sub-question is covered OR the researcher records an explicit 'no source exists' finding for it. Add the ledger table to researcher.md's output format and the exit logic to research-gate.md. The >=5 floor stays as a minimum; coverage becomes the real stop condition.
kept.grounding: Anthropic 'How we built our multi-agent research system' — 'The lead agent... decides whether more research is needed—if so, it can create additional subagents or refine its strategy. Once sufficient information is gathered, the system exits the research loop'; and the judge's 'completeness (are all requested aspects covered?)' rubric dimension (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system).
kept.expected_benefit: Makes the gate adaptive to task shape instead of a blunt count — a step with 5 sub-questions can no longer clear the gate with 5 sources all bearing on one of them. Ties research effort to what the contract actually needs (loop-until-dry), improving downstream plan quality and reducing mid-GENERATE research-on-demand re-spawns.
kept.risk: May add research cycles/cost on multi-question steps, but the researcher fires once per step (rare-event) so cost stays contained. Risk of over-fragmenting a genuinely single-question step — mitigate by letting a 1-sub-question ledger collapse to today's flat 5-source behavior.
kept.fits_constraints: Edits only the researcher role + research-gate mechanics — no re-split, exactly-3 at L3. Cost lands on the Max flat-fee L3 rail. Keeps the JSON gate envelope and 5-file protocol.

verdict.reasoning:
VERIFIED REAL + CONSTRAINT-CLEAN, but incremental — keep at P2, not P1.

Grounding is genuine: I fetched the Anthropic "multi-agent research system" doc and confirmed both quotes verbatim — the LeadResearcher "decides whether more research is needed... Once sufficient information is gathered, the system exits the research loop" (loop-until-dry), and the judge rubric literally lists "completeness (are all requested aspects covered?)". The proposal is a faithful adaptation of two specific, real Anthropic patterns, not cargo-cult.

Current-state description is accurate: researcher.md 168-179 + research-gate.md gate `gate_passed` purely on count (>=5 read-in-full + 10 URLs + recency scan + anchors); nothing maps sources to contract sub-questions. The count-vs-coverage critique is legitimate — 5 sources can all bear on one aspect and still clear the gate. Reframing the stop condition to per-sub-question coverage is a real tightening of the harness's single most-upstream quality gate (weak brief -> weak contract -> the documented F2 "research-on-demand" mid-GENERATE re-spawn).

Constraints: CLEAN. Edits only researcher.md + research-gate.md — exactly-3 at L3, no Explore/harness-verifier re-split, no self-eval impact, runs on the Max flat-fee rail as a rare-event role ($0 metered), keeps the 5-file protocol + JSON envelope, no cloud infra. Over-fragmentation risk is self-mitigated (1-sub-question ledger collapses to today's flat behavior).

Why WEAK/P2 and not STRONG/P1: (1) The mechanism PARTIALLY PRE-EXISTS — deep-tier pass-2 gap analysis (researcher.md 212-218) already enumerates unanswered sub-questions and issues targeted queries; this generalizes+formalizes it to all tiers rather than inventing it. (2) STRESS-TEST DOCTRINE cuts against the proposal's framing that the gate "can no longer clear with 5 sources on one aspect" if that framing assumes a max-effort Opus 4.8 can't track coverage natively across 3-5 sub-questions — that assumption is weak. The surviving justification is auditability (a ledger table Q/A/operator can inspect, matching the project's VERIFICATION_DEFECT/live_check philosophy) + reframing the exit condition (a floor anchors "read 5 and stop"); both are solid but incremental. (3) Leverage is UNQUANTIFIED and the biggest documented research-gate pain (feedback_research_gate: Main slipping/SKIPPING the researcher on 7-of-9 cycles) is a different failure mode this doesn't address. (4) Real COORDINATION COST — research-gate.md explicitly warns "Do not duplicate rules across the three files," and the proposal under-specifies the JSON-envelope coverage field that Q/A actually audits against (it mentions the table but Q/A keys off the envelope programmatically).

Implementation guidance if adopted: lean the rationale on auditability + stop-condition reframe (not "the model can't"), add an explicit coverage field to the JSON envelope so Q/A can gate on it, keep the 1-sub-question collapse, and cross-link (don't duplicate) across researcher.md/research-gate.md/ARCHITECTURE.md.

kept.title: Add a worst-of-N multi-sample / adversarial Q/A verdict for P0/P1 money-path steps (self-consistency within the single Q/A role)
kept.current_state: Q/A is strictly single-shot: qa.md:69 'You run ONCE per cycle (not in a parallel pair anymore).' There is no adversarial / red-team leg and no self-consistency sampling on the verdict. The retry-on-FAIL loop (per-step-protocol.md:140-168) is fix-then-respawn on CHANGED evidence, not an adversarial pass over a claimed PASS. By contrast the Researcher deep-tier DOES have a mandated adversarial pass (researcher.md:212-241) and cites self-consistency evidence (arXiv:2601.20975: n=1→n=8 lifts accuracy 67%→86%, researcher.md:218) — the evaluator gate has no equivalent, despite being the load-bearing false-PASS guard on financial-trading code.
kept.proposed_change: For steps tagged P0/P1 or touching the money-path, run the qa agentType 3x via the Workflow path (Proposal 1) and take the WORST verdict (any FAIL wins; CONDITIONAL dominates PASS). Add an explicit adversarial leg to the LLM-judgment section of qa.md: 'actively construct an input/state that would break the claimed PASS; if you can, the verdict is not PASS.' Gate strictly by priority so cheap P2/P3 steps stay single-shot (cost discipline).
kept.grounding: Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' evaluator-optimizer pattern (https://www.anthropic.com/research/building-effective-agents) and 'Demystifying evals for AI agents' (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/demystifying-evals-for-ai-agents); self-consistency evidence already cited in-repo (arXiv:2601.20975) and devil's-advocate 0%→76% (PMC11615553, researcher.md:230). kept.expected_benefit: Raises evaluator recall on exactly the steps where a false PASS is most expensive — the 345,968-NAV bug class shipped through parse+tests+build and only a live/independent check caught it (qa.md:145-162). A worst-of-3 evaluator makes a single lenient sample non-decisive.
kept.risk: Added latency/tokens — bounded by gating to P0/P1 money-path steps only, and $0 metered via Workflow on the Max rail. A too-aggressive adversarial leg could over-produce FAILs; mitigated by keeping the anti-rubber-stamp criteria concrete and machine-checkable.
kept.fits_constraints: Stays within EXACTLY 3 agents — N instances of the SAME qa role, directly analogous to the researcher deep-tier same-role fork already blessed in per-step-protocol.md:264-279. Cost-appropriate (priority-gated, Max-rail). Reinforces Q/A independence rather than diluting it.

verdict.reasoning:
This is a two-part bundle and the two parts score very differently. VERIFIED constraint-compliant: N same-role qa instances do NOT violate exactly-3-agents — the repo explicitly blesses same-role forking (per-step-protocol.md:273-276 "forks are more instances of the SAME role, never new roles"; researcher.md:242-257 deep-tier fork), and the Workflow-qa launch path is already blessed as within-3-agents, $0 on the Max rail, and stall-immune (auto-memory feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall). All in-repo citations check out (qa.md:69 single-shot; researcher.md:218 arXiv:2601.20975; researcher.md:230 PMC11615553; 345,968-NAV qa.md:153/168; retry-on-FAIL per-step-protocol.md:140-168). Priority tags exist (93 P0 / 152 P1). No self-eval / local-only issues. So no binding constraint is tripped.

WEAK, not STRONG, for three reasons. (1) Grounding for the HEADLINE worst-of-N mechanism is overstated. I fetched "Demystifying evals for AI agents" (real, Anthropic 2026-01-09): it does NOT recommend judge ensembling or worst-of-N verifiers — its pass^k measures AGENT reliability, not evaluator aggregation. "Building Effective Agents" evaluator-optimizer is a generate->evaluate->refine LOOP, which the harness ALREADY implements as the retry-on-FAIL ladder — not verdict ensembling. And the in-repo self-consistency evidence (2601.20975) is MAJORITY-vote (accuracy lift), a different aggregation than worst-of-N (recall). Worst-of-N is a legitimate general high-recall pattern but is NOT what the cited Anthropic docs actually recommend, so the instruction's grounding test is only partially met for part (a). (2) The flagship benefit is misattributed: the 345,968-NAV bug shipped through parse+tests+build and was caught by a LIVE UI capture — a DETERMINISTIC gap already closed by qa.md's live-capture (1c) + runtime-smoke (1d) gates. Three stochastic LLM samples all lacking that capture would PASS identically; worst-of-N would not have caught it. It only helps the narrower "genuine judgment variance on ambiguous evidence" mode. (3) Marginal leverage vs cost: part (a) adds 3x tokens/latency + aggregation logic + a money_path tag that does not exist in masterplan.json, and interacts awkwardly with the existing 3rd-CONDITIONAL auto-FAIL and retry-on-FAIL escalation ladders — on top of already-present anti-leniency machinery (prefer-FAIL qa.md:196, mutation-resistance qa.md:200-201, code-review-trading-domain anti-rubber-stamp skill).

Net recommendation (why still KEEP, at P2): part (b), the adversarial red-team leg in qa.md's LLM-judgment section ("actively construct an input/state that would break the claimed PASS"), IS a genuine, cheap, always-on, well-grounded win — it ports the researcher's already-blessed adversarial pass (researcher.md:212-241, devil's-advocate PMC11615553) to the load-bearing false-PASS gate, reinforces Q/A independence, and adds real content beyond the existing mutation-resistance check (red-teaming the PASS CLAIM vs. testing the artifact's guards). Codify part (b) as an S-effort qa.md prompt edit (separation-of-duties: authoring session must not self-eval work depending on it; takes effect next roster snapshot). DROP or strictly defer part (a) worst-of-N; if kept at all, scope it to genuine-ambiguity P0 steps only, and do not sell it on the 345,968-NAV example. P2 (not P1) because the target is the highest-stakes gate but the net marginal hardening after existing gates/anti-rubber-stamp is modest and the headline mechanism is oversold; not P3 because a real adversarial-judging technique on the false-PASS gate is more than a nice-to-have.

kept.title: Add an independent adversarial evaluator gate before skill_optimizer.apply_modification (evaluator-optimizer for the prompt self-improvement loop)
kept.current_state: skill_optimizer.propose_skill_modification (skill_optimizer.py:300-395) is a one-shot Gemini proposal (temperature 0.7) parsed with prose-JSON _extract_json. Before the edit is written, apply_modification (line 399+) only runs a mechanical old_text-uniqueness check plus passes_simplicity_criterion (line 570). There is NO independent LLM reviewer checking whether the diff weakens a constraint, changes a {{variable}} or output schema it promised not to, or misdescribes itself -- i.e. the generator judges its own work, the exact anti-pattern the codebase forbids elsewhere. The correct template already exists in Layer-4 at directive_review.py:73-261: a separate judge, the proposer's self-score hidden, a 5-dim rubric (clarity/alignment/safety/proportionality/factuality), fail-CLOSED on any error.
kept.proposed_change: Insert a directive_review-style independent evaluator between propose_skill_modification and apply_modification: score the proposed diff on alignment (preserves placeholders/schema), safety (no constraint weakened vs the RULES the proposer was given), proportionality, and factuality (description matches the actual diff); reject-and-skip below threshold; fail-closed on LLM error. Reuse review_directive_diff's structure to avoid new code.
kept.grounding: Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' evaluator-optimizer workflow (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents) and the multi-agent research system's 'separate the agent doing the work from the agent judging it'. The repo already ships this pattern for directives (directive_review.py) but skill_optimizer -- which edits live analysis-agent prompts -- lacks it.
kept.expected_benefit: Turns the prompt-self-improvement loop into a true generator+independent-evaluator loop, catching constraint-weakening or self-misdescribing edits before they touch skills/*.md (which drive per-ticker Layer-1 analysis).
kept.risk: Low. skill_optimizer runs weekly (MIN_SKILL_OPT_INTERVAL_DAYS=7) on Gemini, so the extra judge call is rare and cheap. Must ensure fail-closed (reject on judge error) so a transient LLM failure never auto-applies an unreviewed edit.
kept.fits_constraints: Rare-event, Gemini-cheap -> respects Layer-2 cost sensitivity. Reuses an existing in-repo pattern; no new infra; strengthens (not bypasses) evaluator independence.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP — verified real, constraint-clean, closes a genuine self-judging gap. All current-state line refs check out exactly: propose_skill_modification (skill_optimizer.py:300-395) is a one-shot Gemini/temp-0.7 proposal; apply_modification (399-451) guards ONLY mechanically (old_text-in-content 410, uniqueness 417, load_skill validation 435 which catches unloadable files, NOT semantic weakening); passes_simplicity_criterion (570) is pure line-count math. The propose prompt's own RULES (no {{var}} change, no schema change — 336-338) are self-policed by the generator; nothing independent verifies compliance, and apply_modification does a GLOBAL content.replace (424) so it does not even bound the edit to `## Prompt Template`. The edit is git-committed (444-450) and, since immediate delta is structurally ~always 0 (acknowledged 765-770), lands in PENDING (785) and stays LIVE — so the delta-discard is a near-useless near-term guard and the mechanical checks are effectively the only pre-live gate. This IS the generator-judges-own-work anti-pattern the codebase forbids elsewhere.

Reuse template is real and matches: directive_review.py ships separate judge + hidden self-score (77-78) + 5-dim clarity/alignment/safety/proportionality/factuality rubric (42-48; alignment="preserves floors", safety="does NOT weaken any constraint" 106-107) + fail-CLOSED on any error (169-180). Grounding verified via WebFetch: "Building Effective Agents" evaluator-optimizer ("one LLM generates while another provides evaluation and feedback in a loop… effective when we have clear evaluation criteria") plus the genuine "separate the agent doing the work from the agent judging it" principle. Not cargo-cult — it extends a pattern the repo already runs for directives to the one Layer-2 loop that edits LIVE per-ticker analysis prompts and lacks it.

Constraints clean: Layer-2 only (Layer-3 3-agent rule untouched); Gemini + rare (MetaCoordinator gates skill_opt to >=7-day intervals AND accuracy<0.55, meta_coordinator.py:122/176-186) so negligible cost, off the $0-metered Claude Code rail and off the per-ticker budget; strengthens evaluator independence; local/paper, no new infra.

P2 not P1: bounded leverage — loop is rare/gated/conditional and edits are git-revertible; a weakened prompt degrades signal quality but trades still clear the downstream risk gates (kill-switch/PBO/DD caps), so blast radius is analysis quality, not capital control. Implementation notes for whoever codifies it: (1) prefer cheap DETERMINISTIC checks first — placeholder set unchanged, JSON-schema block untouched, edit lands inside `## Prompt Template` (fixes the unbounded-replace gap) — then the LLM judge only for the semantic dims (safety/factuality); (2) pin the judge to Gemini rather than directive_review's Anthropic-Sonnet-first path (_call_llm_for_review 130-147) to honor $0-metered-first; (3) fail-closed = skip the edit, which is safe since skipping an optimization is harmless. Solid defense-in-depth on a real self-judging gap; effort M is honest (adapt the pattern, not call review_directive_diff verbatim since it takes a DirectiveVersion).

kept.title: StructuredOutput tool for harness phase verdicts (machine-readable cycle state)
kept.current_state: Harness cycles (plan → generate → evaluate) produce freeform markdown files (contract.md, experiment_results.md, evaluator_critique.md) + status flip in .claude/masterplan.json. Cycle-to-cycle state inference (is Q/A PASS? are there violated_criteria?) requires manual parsing of markdown + reading JSON. No schema enforcement. Masterplan step 67.4 live_check_gate is a bash hook that checks file existence, not content shape. No machine-verified verdict schema.
kept.proposed_change: Main harness phase returns structured verdict JSON via StructuredOutput tool after Q/A Workflow completes. Schema: { ok: bool, verdict: 'PASS'|'CONDITIONAL'|'FAIL', violated_criteria: string[], violation_details: string, checks_run: { syntax: bool, file_exists: bool, live_command: bool, linter: bool }, certified_fallback: bool, step_id: string, cycle_num: int }. Verdict persisted to handoff/current/evaluator_critique.json alongside markdown. Masterplan status flip reads the JSON for deterministic gate (not markdown parsing). Auto-commit-and-push hook reads JSON to decide CONDITIONAL retry eligibility (cycle-2 gate: 3rd-CONDITIONAL-in-a-row auto-FAIL per CLAUDE.md).
kept.grounding: Anthropic Claude Code StructuredOutput tool is first-class (tools-reference). Python SDK + TypeScript SDK both ship it. Pattern: ask Claude for a task, capture the return value as structured JSON, feed it into a state machine. This is the core of Anthropic's 'Building Effective Agents' + 'Harness design for long-running apps' (control flow via structured returns, not LLM-decided prose). Pyfinagent already uses Gemini JSON schema enforcement for Layer-1 agent outputs; StructuredOutput is the Claude equivalent.
kept.expected_benefit: Cycle-to-cycle automation gains: (1) deterministic retry gate (cycle-2 on CONDITIONAL without human reading markdown), (2) harness_log.md audit-ability (verdict JSON is immutable per step, facilitates burn-down audits like phase-69), (3) integration with operator dashboards (masterplan JSON + verdict JSON form a complete state tuple, can feed CI/CD or Slack notifications). Reduces markdown parsing fragility. Future: allows automated harness rule changes (e.g., auto-FAIL on 3rd CONDITIONAL) without manually editing hooks.
kept.risk: Schema churn: if verdict shape changes per-step, masterplan JSON parsing breaks until operators re-run. Mitigation: version the schema (schema_version: 1) and provide a migration script. Dual-write risk: if Main writes both markdown and JSON, they can diverge if one write fails. Mitigation: write JSON first, then markdown as a report derivative. Low operational risk if the schema is locked down before phase-68 launch.
kept.fits_constraints: File-based handoffs still primary: JSON files live in handoff/current/ same as .md files, subject to archive-handoff hook. Separation of duties: verdict JSON is Q/A output, not Main judgment. No agent re-split. Preserves 5-file protocol (contract.md, results.md, critique.md exist + new critique.json). Max-plan: StructuredOutput is stdlib, zero cost.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP the narrow core, strip the gold-plating. Grounding is REAL: StructuredOutput/Workflow structured-output is a live Claude Code / Agent-SDK feature (I am invoking StructuredOutput right now to return this verdict; the deferred toolset exposes Workflow primitives), and auto-memory feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall proves the Workflow `agent(prompt,{agentType:'qa',schema})` path ran 110-290s in-project where 4 Agent-tool Q/A spawns hung. Minor mis-framing ("stdlib", vague "tools-reference") but substance holds — not a reject-on-fabrication.

WEAK/P2 rather than STRONG/P1 because the proposal is largely redundant and over-scoped: (1) qa.md lines 213-249 ALREADY specify the verdict JSON contract (ok/verdict/violated_criteria/certified_fallback/checks_run) — the proposal reinvents ~90% of it; (2) the auto-memory ALREADY prescribes the Workflow-structured-return path with "Main persists the returned verdict" — the only genuine delta is codifying it into run_harness.py + the protocol, which is useful housekeeping of a known operator-blessed pattern, not a new capability; (3) the headline benefit ("deterministic gate not markdown parsing / reduces parsing fragility") solves a non-problem — the masterplan status flip is a deliberate Main action and the hooks (live_check_gate.py, auto-commit-and-push.sh) parse masterplan.json + file EXISTENCE, never evaluator_critique.md content, so there is no fragile markdown parser to replace.

Strip before keeping: (a) making auto-commit-and-push.sh read JSON to gate CONDITIONAL retry eligibility adds branching to a fail-open hook that CLAUDE.md insists must never break the Write and that feedback_auto_commit_hook_stalls shows already stalls — net risk up, and the 3rd-CONDITIONAL auto-FAIL already lives correctly in qa.md via harness_log.md; (b) schema-version + migration script for ephemeral per-step handoff JSON that the archive-handoff hook rotates = cargo-cult; (c) CI/CD / operator-dashboard futures drift toward infra this paper-only, local-only, single-Mac project does not have.

Constraint check: exactly-3-L3-agents preserved (Q/A stays Q/A via a different launch path, no re-split); $0-metered (Max-plan Claude Code rail); file-based 5-file protocol preserved (JSON added alongside .md, still archived). Binding guardrail on implementation: it MUST be Q/A-Workflow-RETURNS-the-verdict then Main transcribes to evaluator_critique.{md,json} — NOT Main deciding the verdict (the title's literal "Main returns the verdict via StructuredOutput" would be forbidden self-eval; fits_constraints already commits to the safe reading, keep it binding). Net: reduce to "first-class Workflow-structured-output Q/A path in run_harness.py + protocol, persist the returned verdict JSON beside the markdown" and P2 it.

kept.title: Loop-until-dry completeness-critic pass for audit-class steps
kept.current_state: There is no loop-until-dry / completeness critic in the harness. The Researcher deep-tier has a scan→gap→adversarial multi-pass (researcher.md:212-241) but it is researcher-only, opt-in, and about SOURCES, not finding-coverage. Phase-69 was a 50-finding audit whose completeness was guaranteed by a MANUAL 1..50 checksum in the coverage table (harness_log Cycle 86), not a mechanized re-scan-until-empty loop. This very meta-audit is exactly the kind of multi-dimension sweep where a silent gap (a missed capability) is the failure mode with no forcing function against it.
kept.proposed_change: Add an 'audit-class step' variant to per-step-protocol.md: after the first Researcher/Q/A pass, run a bounded completeness-critic loop — re-spawn the qa (or researcher) agentType via Workflow with 'given findings already recorded in <file>, return {new_findings:[...], exhausted:bool} — list anything MISSED' — and loop until exhausted=true or a hard ceiling of 3 passes; terminate on an empty delta. This is the evaluator-optimizer termination-on-criteria-met applied to audit coverage, and the empty-delta stop is the machine-checkable termination condition.
kept.grounding: Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' — 'the loop terminates when the criteria are met or iterations are exhausted' (https://www.anthropic.com/research/building-effective-agents); Anthropic multi-agent research — 'the LeadResearcher decides whether more research is needed—if so it can create additional subagents' (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system); researcher.md deep-tier multi-pass precedent.
kept.expected_benefit: Converts 'the agent claimed complete coverage' into a mechanized exhaustion proof for audits/burn-downs, attacking the silent-drop class the phase-69.4 coverage checksum guards by hand today.
kept.risk: Loop cost or non-termination — bounded by the hard 3-pass ceiling and the empty-delta stop. Only applies to explicitly audit-tagged steps, so it doesn't tax normal cycles.
kept.fits_constraints: No new agents — bounded re-invocation of the SAME researcher/qa roles. Preserves the 5-file protocol (findings accumulate in the handoff files). Max-rail $0.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP as a solid, constraint-clean, real upgrade — but with material caveats, hence WEAK/P2 rather than STRONG/P1.

CURRENT-STATE VERIFIED (against the live repo): (a) researcher.md:212-241 is exactly the deep-tier scan→gap→adversarial multi-pass, and it is source-coverage, researcher-only, tier-gated — the proposal correctly frames it as NOT finding-coverage; (b) harness_log Cycle 86 (phase-69.4, lines 27340-27350) confirms completeness was a MANUAL "exhaustive 1..50 subsystem checksum" in a coverage table, re-checked by an independent Q/A — no mechanized re-scan loop; (c) per-step-protocol.md's only loop is retry-on-FAIL (max-3), which corrects FAIL/CONDITIONAL verdicts, not coverage. So "no completeness critic exists today" is accurate.

CONSTRAINTS — all pass: re-invokes the SAME qa/researcher roles (no 4th agent, no Explore/harness-verifier re-split); $0 Max-rail via the Workflow launch path (which composes with the already-discovered feedback_workflow_qa_when_subagents_stall reliability mitigation); the critic is an INDEPENDENT qa/researcher instance, so it is not Main self-evaluation; findings accumulate in the handoff files, preserving the 5-file protocol; local-only. Not verdict-shopping either — each pass asks a different question ("what's missing?") and mutates the evidence file, so the fresh-respawn is the legitimate cycle-2 shape, not a re-litigation of an unchanged verdict.

GROUNDING — survives but is imperfect (verified by fetching the live article). The multi-agent-research quote is real (it is quoted verbatim in this project's own CLAUDE.md). The "Building Effective Agents" quote is NOT verbatim: the article actually says autonomous agents use "stopping conditions (such as a maximum number of iterations)" and that evaluator-optimizer is "particularly effective when we have clear evaluation criteria." The bounded-loop mechanism is genuinely supported; but open-ended "list anything MISSED" completeness lacks the clear criteria the evaluator-optimizer pattern presupposes, so on codification the grounding should be reframed to autonomous-loop + multi-agent-research (drop the evaluator-optimizer framing), and the exact quote should be corrected to the real wording.

WHY P2 NOT P1: real recurring audit-class workload (phase-69's 50-finding burn-down, the 280-agent 2026-07-10 ultracode audit, this meta-sweep itself), and a genuine gap — the manual checksum only works when the denominator is pre-enumerated (phase-69's 50 came from the upstream audit); for a net-new audit with an unknown total it cannot help, which is exactly the case the loop targets. But frequency is low, a partial version already exists (deep-tier multi-pass), and the failure mode was in fact caught in phase-69 by existing machinery. WHY NOT STRONG: the expected_benefit oversells — "mechanized exhaustion PROOF" is wrong; a self-critic shares the first pass's blind spots, so empty-delta is corroborating EVIDENCE, not proof (multi-sample research supports it recovering real misses, but not proving exhaustion). Codify with three edits: (1) reframe grounding as above, (2) downgrade "proof" to "evidence," (3) scope the variant to net-new/unknown-denominator audits where the manual checksum cannot substitute. Effort is genuinely ~M (a per-step-protocol.md audit-class variant + a Main-driven Workflow loop; no run_harness.py rewrite required), non-termination is bounded, and it taxes only audit-tagged steps.

kept.title: Scheduled (weekly) report-only harness self-audit that runs the stress-test doctrine automatically — and prune the dormant self-evaluating driver
kept.current_state: No ACTIVE scheduled self-audit of the Layer-3 harness. The only scheduled harness driver (scripts/mas_harness/run_cycle.sh + cycle_prompt.md) targets a STALE May go-live checklist; its launchd plists are all inactive (disabled.com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist.bak, *.plist.bak-harness-ABCD), mas-harness.log is empty, and — critically — cycle_prompt.md Step 4 (lines 62-66) SELF-EVALUATES: the same `claude -p` session that generates also writes evaluator_critique.md, violating the no-self-eval rule. The CLAUDE.md stress-test doctrine ('re-run a representative step WITHOUT the harness on each model release … prune stale scaffolding') is enforced only manually/ad-hoc. Active launchd agents (autoresearch, ablation) are Layer-4, not harness self-audit.
kept.proposed_change: Add a weekly report-only self-audit via the scheduled-task surface (local launchd `claude -p`, or the `schedule`/routine skill) that: (1) runs scripts/qa/verify_qa_roster_live.sh to confirm the roster is EXACTLY 3 agents on live Opus pins; (2) grep-checks protocol invariants (5-file presence, no re-split, no stale model pin past a revert date — the class the SessionStart tripwire only WARNS on); (3) spawns an INDEPENDENT qa agentType (Workflow) to critique the last N cycles' harness-compliance — never self-eval. Concurrently retire or clearly mark-dead the self-evaluating cycle_prompt.md and the no-op `_default_spawn_researcher` stub (run_harness.py:1044-1081) as stale scaffolding.
kept.grounding: Claude Code headless/scheduled tasks + GitHub-Actions cron (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/github-actions) and the `schedule`/`loop` skills available this session; CLAUDE.md stress-test doctrine grounded in Anthropic harness-design (https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/harness-design-long-running-apps) — 'those assumptions are worth stress testing'.
kept.expected_benefit: Makes the stress-test/anti-drift doctrine self-enforcing instead of drift-prone; automatically catches roster/pin drift (e.g. a model pin outliving its revert date) and protocol slips; removes a dormant driver that actively models the forbidden self-eval pattern.
kept.risk: Another autonomous loop to babysit, and the documented background-agent self-resume/unauthorized-push risk (auto-memory feedback_background_agent_resumption_risk). Mitigate hard: report-only — NO masterplan writes, NO git push, independent verdict emitted to a log/Slack only.
kept.fits_constraints: Local-only (launchd on Peder's Mac; no fleet/cloud). $0 on Max/Opus. Keeps EXACTLY 3 agents (self-audit spawns the same qa role). The report-only constraint respects 'self-evaluation forbidden' and the no-unauthorized-write rules.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP but heavily descoped; WEAK/P3. I verified the proposal's factual spine against the live repo and it is largely real: scripts/mas_harness/cycle_prompt.md Step 4 (lines 62-66) genuinely models the FORBIDDEN self-eval pattern (same `claude -p` session generates in Step 3 and writes evaluator_critique.md in Step 4), it targets the stale May Go-Live Checklist, last ran 2026-04-19, and `launchctl list` confirms mas-harness is NOT loaded (only .bak/disabled plists). The stress-test doctrine and its Anthropic-harness-design quote are real (verbatim in CLAUDE.md), and `claude -p` headless + local launchd are real, already-used mechanisms — so the LOCAL implementation path is constraint-clean ($0/Max, local-only, no re-split, spawns the same qa role so still exactly-3).

But two things sink it from a STRONG win to a nice-to-have. (1) FACTUAL ERRORS undercut the pruning half: "mas-harness.log is empty" is false (2.9MB, just stale), and `_default_spawn_researcher` (run_harness.py:1044-1081) is NOT dead scaffolding — it is wired in as the live default at line 1122, is intentional deterministic-harness design, and backs the documented F2 research-on-demand contract; "retiring/marking it dead" would breach CLAUDE.md failure-discipline. The author conflated live design with dead code. (2) The HEADLINE — a new weekly autonomous `claude -p` self-audit loop — is low-leverage and risky. Its flagship catches (roster != 3, model pin past a revert date, 5-file/no-resplit invariants) are ALREADY covered per-session by the existing SessionStart tripwire (which fires more often than weekly and has already correctly resolved the exact cited case: researcher.md/qa.md are `model: opus` today, post-Fable-revert) and per-step by Q/A. So incremental catch-rate is marginal. Worse, a new `--dangerously-skip-permissions` loop re-introduces the precise background-agent unauthorized-write risk that already burned the operator (reverted commit ad349f57); the proposed "report-only prompt" mitigation is exactly the one auto-memory feedback_background_agent_resumption_risk records as ALREADY FAILED ("review-only prompts are not enforcement") — a prompt cannot bind a skip-permissions session away from git push. Plus babysit cost on a machine with documented dead webhook paging.

Grounding note: the GitHub-Actions-cron and `schedule`/cloud-routine citations are real Claude Code features but CLOUD — using them would violate local-only; only the launchd path is compliant, so those citations are loose/irrelevant, not disqualifying.

Net: KEEP the verified nugget, REJECT the mechanism as framed. Descope to (a) DELETE the stale self-evaluating cycle_prompt.md + run_cycle.sh + the .bak/disabled mas-harness plists (pure stress-test-doctrine housekeeping; removes a latent forbidden-pattern trap at ~0 risk), and (b) add ~5 grep asserts (roster==3 on opus pins; no `model:` pin past its revert date) to the ALREADY-RUNNING per-session SessionStart hook — NOT a new autonomous loop. Do NOT touch `_default_spawn_researcher` (live F2 design, misdiagnosed). If any scheduled surface is ever used it must be launchd-local, never the cloud schedule/GH-Actions path. P3: the drift-catch is already covered per-session/per-step and the residual is inert-cleanup + a couple hook lines, so it is a low-effort hygiene item, not a high-leverage upgrade.

kept.title: Pin Main's Layer-3 effort deterministically via CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL (and resolve xhigh-vs-max)
kept.current_state: .claude/settings.json sets effortLevel:"xhigh" (a persisted key, confirmed by model-config doc which names "The persisted effortLevel setting") and env only carries CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 -- no CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL. Main (Opus 4.8) therefore runs one tier BELOW the two subagents, which are both effort:max. There is no env-level pin, so headless/cron/background harness runs are exposed to Opus 4.8's default-`high` hold.
kept.proposed_change: Add "CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL":"xhigh" (or "max") to the settings.json env block so Main's effort is deterministic across interactive, headless (-p/cron), and background-agent harness runs. CRITICAL FINDING: if the operator wants Main at max -- consistent with Researcher+Q/A already at max and the "run at MAX effort" directive -- the settings effortLevel:"max" key will NOT persist it; the env var is the ONLY documented way to hold max. Recommend max given the Layer-3 flat-fee Max rail + rare-event (per-step) firing, gated by the calibration eval proposed separately.
kept.grounding: platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/effort (Opus 4.8: "Start with xhigh for coding and agentic use cases... step up to max only when your evals show measurable headroom at xhigh"). code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config, "Adjust effort level": "max ... applies to the current session only, except when set through the CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL environment variable"; "The default effort is high on ... Opus 4.8 ... held across sessions until you make an explicit effort choice"; a non-interactive /effort "can't release the model-default hold ... on Opus 4.8 it reports Not applied."
kept.expected_benefit: Guarantees Main never silently runs at Opus 4.8's default `high` in headless/cron harness runs (the exact silent-effort-drop risk this audit targets, applied to the orchestrator), and unlocks the only reliable path to run Main at max -- currently impossible through the settings key alone.
kept.risk: Low. The env var is documented and version-tolerant (invalid values are ignored/warned). Verify via a headless --print run reading the result modelUsage/effort. If max overthinks structured planning, revert to xhigh via the same key.
kept.fits_constraints: Layer-3 only; flat-fee Max rail so no metered cost; does not touch the 3-agent split or Q/A independence. Fully compliant.

verdict.reasoning:
VERIFIED against source. Current-state claims confirmed in .claude/settings.json (effortLevel:"xhigh" line 2; env block only CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1, no CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL). Grounding is 100% real, not hallucinated — I fetched both cited docs and confirmed the quotes verbatim: model-config (code.claude.com) line 486 "set effortLevel to low, medium, high, or xhigh... max and ultracode are session-only and are not accepted here"; line 443 "max... applies to the current session only, except when set through the CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL environment variable"; line 439 "default effort is high on... Opus 4.8"; line 443 non-interactive /effort "reports Not applied"; effort doc (platform.claude.com) Opus 4.8 "Start with xhigh... step up to max only when your evals show measurable headroom at xhigh." The CRITICAL FINDING (settings.json cannot hold max; env var is the only documented persistent path to max) is correct and non-obvious — genuine research value.

KEEP=true but WEAK/P3, and ONLY the xhigh env pin — the max half must be stripped. Two adversarial defects the proposal glosses over:

(1) HEADLINE RISK IS OVERSTATED. The proposal's own cited doc gives the precedence chain (model-config line 489): "env var > your configured level > model default." settings.json effortLevel:"xhigh" IS a "configured level" that already outranks the model default, so Main most likely already runs xhigh in headless/cron. The residual gap is only a documentation-ambiguity edge case (settings.json is absent from line 441's "explicit effort choice" examples, which name only interactive /effort and --effort). No project incident of a silent effort drop has ever been observed (the real recurring pain is subagent end-flush stalls, unrelated). So this is belt-and-suspenders hardening against a largely pre-mitigated risk, not a fix for a live defect — hence low leverage.

(2) MAX IS NOT JUSTIFIED. Bumping Main to max contradicts the cited Opus 4.8 guidance ("step up to max only when your evals show measurable headroom at xhigh") and the doc's own max caveat ("on some structured-output... tasks it can lead to overthinking") — Main's role is orchestration/structured-planning-heavy, exactly the overthinking risk. The proposal itself concedes max is "gated by the calibration eval proposed separately," i.e. not adopt-now. Adopt the env pin at xhigh only.

Why keep at all: it's a one-line, zero-maintenance, fail-safe change (invalid values ignored/warned), $0 metered on the flat-fee Max Claude Code rail, on a real headless/cron/background deployment surface, that removes the documented precedence/hold ambiguity and is the only path to max should a future eval justify it. CONSTRAINT CHECK — fully compliant: Layer-3 only, no metered cost, does not touch the exactly-3-agent split, Q/A independence, or the 5-file protocol; no agent-.md edit so no roster-snapshot concern. Not a REJECT (grounding real, no constraint violated, near-zero cost so benefit/cost ratio is fine), but low-leverage vs current state = P3.

kept.title: Tighten the Layer-3 fallbackModel chain: drop the primary-equal first hop, add an availability floor
kept.current_state: .claude/settings.json sets fallbackModel: ["claude-opus-4-8", "claude-sonnet-5"]. The first entry equals Main's primary model (Opus 4.8). The model-config doc says chains are "capped at three models after duplicate removal," and falling back from an overloaded Opus 4.8 to Opus 4.8 re-hits the same overload -- so the first hop is effectively a no-op and the real chain is just [sonnet-5]. The SessionStart tripwire already documents that the chain only covers OVERLOAD-class failures.
kept.proposed_change: Set fallbackModel: ["claude-sonnet-5", "claude-haiku-4-5"] -- remove the redundant opus-4-8, keep sonnet-5 as the primary fallback, add haiku-4-5 as a last-resort availability floor. Effort continuity is already verified: Sonnet 5 supports xhigh per the effort doc AND the llm_client xhigh-guard (llm_client.py:1524) includes claude-sonnet-5, so Main's xhigh survives the sonnet-5 hop; on the haiku floor Claude Code clamps to the highest supported level, acceptable for a rare availability-only hop.
kept.grounding: code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config, "Fallback model chains": "When the primary model is overloaded, unavailable, or returns another non-retryable server error ... Authentication, billing, rate-limit, request-size, and transport errors never trigger a switch"; "Chains are capped at three models after duplicate removal"; array form + the doc's own example ["claude-sonnet-5", "claude-haiku-4-5"]; "Adjust effort level" (clamp to highest supported level at or below the one you set).
kept.expected_benefit: A genuine 2-deep availability chain for the long-running harness instead of one wasted hop, with Main's effort preserved on the primary fallback -- better overload resilience for unattended cron runs.
kept.risk: Very low. Config-only, exercised only on the overload path. Verify the array is accepted at session start (no --debug warning).
kept.fits_constraints: Layer-3 session config only; fallback runs on the subscription session so no metered cost; no impact on the 3-agent split.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP but low-leverage. Every grounding claim verifies verbatim against the live code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config: "Chains are capped at three models after duplicate removal"; the exact trigger split (overload/unavailable/non-retryable-server-error switch; auth/billing/rate-limit/request-size/transport never); the effort clamp to "highest supported level at or below the one you set"; and the effort table explicitly lists "Sonnet 5, Opus 4.8, and Opus 4.7 -> low/medium/high/xhigh/max" so Main's xhigh survives the sonnet-5 hop with no clamp. Notably the proposed value is LITERALLY the doc's own canonical example ["claude-sonnet-5","claude-haiku-4-5"]. Current-state also verified: settings.json:3 is exactly ["claude-opus-4-8","claude-sonnet-5"] with the first entry == primary; the SessionStart tripwire does document overload-only; llm_client.py:1524 does carry sonnet-5 in the xhigh set. Constraints all clean: fallback runs on the Max-subscription CC rail (sonnet+haiku included) = $0 metered; 3-agent split, local-only, no-self-eval untouched; real feature (v2.1.166). Effort S, risk ~0.

Why P3 not higher: the change is correct but marginal. (1) It is ORTHOGONAL to the harness's actual documented pain -- subagent end-flush stalls are client-side hangs, not overload/server errors, so fallbackModel never fires on them (the doc's trigger list excludes transport/hang-class failures). (2) The current chain already reaches sonnet-5 today; net new value is only a haiku availability floor for the rare BOTH-opus-and-sonnet-overloaded case plus removing a post-Fable-revert cosmetic redundancy (first entry == primary). Real for an unattended cron app, but not a leverage driver.

Two non-fatal imprecisions to flag if codified: (a) the "duplicate removal removes the PRIMARY" reading is not unambiguously stated -- the doc phrasing more naturally means dedup WITHIN the fallback list; but the conclusion is robust either way (opus->opus fallback is either deduped or an immediate retry of a still-overloaded model, so ~1 useful hop now vs 2 proposed). (b) llm_client.py:1524 governs Layer-2 in-app metered calls, NOT the Main CC session's effort handling (the CC binary owns that), so citing it as proof "Main's xhigh survives" is slightly misdirected -- but the load-bearing proof (effort table) is correct, so the claim stands. Since cost/risk are ~0, even marginal benefit clears the bar; keep as a low-priority polish, do not prioritize over anything that touches the stall class.

verdict.reasoning:
KEEP as low-leverage config hygiene. All claims verified against the live tree.

GROUNDING IS REAL (not fabricated): git 0975a7ea (phase-67.4 "Fable pins back to Opus 4.8") flipped model: fable->opus; masterplan 67.4 status=done (67.1-67.6 + phase-69 all done). This is not the high-value harness upgrade (that would be codifying the Workflow-Q/A fallback path into run_harness.py). Cheap and correct, so keep and do when convenient.
