verdict.reasoning:
REJECT — the proposal's central premise is falsified by the code. Its factual current-state claims are accurate: EFFORT_DEFAULTS (model_tiers.py:261-271) does pin mas_communication/mas_main/mas_qa/mas_research all to "max"; the 23.2.2 "STEP-SCOPED override ... revert after closure" comment with revert targets (communication=low, main=xhigh, qa=high, research=medium) exists verbatim (lines 255-260, ~2mo stale); the phase-59.1 test asserts resolve_effort("mas_main")=="max"; and the effort-doc quote ("Reserve max for genuinely frontier problems...") is genuine (quoted verbatim in-code at 258-259 and in CLAUDE.md).

BUT the causal claim — "un-analyzed max spend on metered per-ticker roles" and "overthinking risk on structured-output MAS verdicts/routing" — is FALSE. The mas_* effort=max values are DEAD CONFIG at runtime. resolve_effort() is consumed in exactly one place, the llm_client.py ClaudeClient wrapper (1509,1513). The Layer-2 MAS orchestrator bypasses that wrapper entirely: _get_client() (multi_agent_orchestrator.py:184-207) returns the raw anthropic.Anthropic() SDK, and both call sites — _call_agent (1008-1013) and _call_agent_with_tools (1120-1127) — send only model/max_tokens/system/messages(/tools/thinking). No output_config/effort is ever passed (in this 1490-line file "effort" appears only in comments at 125, 1105). The only _role values routed through the effort-resolving wrapper are lite_trader/lite_risk_judge (autonomous_loop.py:2468,2508), NOT any mas_* role; resolve_model("mas_*") is used only for the MODEL id in agent_definitions.py, never effort.

Consequences that sink the proposal: (1) effort=max is never transmitted to the API for these roles, so there is NO live max-effort spend to remove and NO doc-warned overthinking-on-max on MAS output — both stated benefits are illusory. (2) The prescribed "M"-effort per-ticker token-delta cost analysis (max vs high/medium) would measure a knob with zero runtime effect — wasted work. (3) The cited effort-doc warning is real but INAPPLICABLE here because the premise (these roles run at max) is false at the runtime level; per the task's grounding-verification rule, the grounding does not support the claimed benefit.

What actually survives is a doc/config-hygiene contradiction (the 23.2.2 revert-intent comment vs the still-max dict; the line 246-254 "Applied below" prose describing the lower tiers while the dict says max; CLAUDE.md describing mas_main as xhigh while code says max). That is genuine but low-leverage (P3) and the proposal misframes it as a cost/quality problem, which would send a future session down a measure-an-inert-variable path and could entrench the incorrect "effort=max is live for MAS" assumption baked into the line-125 comment.

The genuinely valuable finding underneath is a DIFFERENT, better-scoped proposal: the effort-config-vs-runtime disconnect — Layer-2 MAS config/comments claim effort=max but the raw-SDK call path never passes output_config.effort, so the setting is silently inert. The correct fix is a binary decision (either wire output_config.effort into the orchestrator's messages.create calls, or delete the dead mas_* EFFORT_DEFAULTS entries and correct the comments to say effort is not applied on the raw-SDK path) — not a cost analysis of an unused knob. No binding constraint is violated by the proposal (it doesn't touch L3, self-eval, agent count, or infra), but it fails the "benefit marginal/illusory vs cost" and "grounding doesn't say what is claimed" bars. Reject as written; reformulate as the config-vs-runtime reconciliation if pursued.

GROUNDING VERIFIED REAL (not a reject-on-fabrication): fetched platform.claude.com/.../effort in full — "up to `max` only when your evals show measurable headroom at `xhigh`" is the Opus-4.7 guidance, which the doc says "also applies to Claude Opus 4.8"; "Test your use case… Evaluate performance… before deploying" is verbatim Best-practice #3; the max-cost/overthinking warning is real. The one residual quality risk the doc raises (overthinking) is explicitly scoped to "structured-output or less intelligence-sensitive tasks," i.e. the opposite of Q/A adversarial judgment and Researcher multi-source synthesis. So the guidance's protective purpose is already satisfied and its quality-caveat is doc-scoped-away for these role types.

INVALID MEASUREMENT. Scoped to "ONE representative step," the eval is N=1 per role: an xhigh-vs-max quality delta on a single Researcher brief / Q/A verdict / Main plan is within LLM run-to-run variance. Replacing a well-reasoned, documented assertion with a coin-flip measurement is worse, not better — it invites acting on noise (contra the operator's own "measure before you fix" discipline, which is about money-engine paper_* metrics, not internal knobs). Only the Q/A-planted-violation leg is cleanly measurable (binary catch/miss); the Researcher/Main "quality delta" needs a rubric+judge that is unspecified and, if Main judges, brushes self-eval.

WRONG TARGET + TREADMILL. The genuinely cost-relevant effort question is LAYER-2 (metered, per-ticker, all mas_* already at `max` per model_tiers.py:262-265) — that is where the doc's cost warning actually bites. This proposal aims its eval at L3 (flat-fee, where the answer changes no spend) and only hand-waves the L2 tie-in. Meanwhile the actual OBSERVED Q/A pain is end-flush STALLING (6x on 2026-07-11, transport, already mitigated via the Workflow structured-output path) — an effort-tier A/B does not touch it. Add M implementation + a recurring per-release re-run (Mythos/Sonnet 5 already shipped) for an outcome the proposal itself predicts "worst case confirms the current tiers," plus fable-pin instability (xhigh→high silent downgrade). Net: real and harmless, but low-leverage scaffolding pointed at the layer where cost is moot. Salvageable kernel worth a separate, cheaper proposal: a standalone Q/A planted-violation regression decoupled from effort tuning, and/or an L2 cost-right-sizing eval where the doc's guidance actually applies.
